title: The Officer Agents Are Expected
description: On pages 5–6, this part focuses on the officer agents are expected, keeping the scene centered on the conflict, choices, and stakes of the Alabasta arc.
scene: The Officer Agents Are Expected. Baroque Works’ senior agents are summoned while the Straw Hats and Vivi continue moving through the desert toward the heart of Alabasta’s crisis. This section presents the immediate scene beat before the dialogue continues.

dialogue.speaker: Miss All Sunday
dialogue.text: Whoa! What the heck is that!? A turtle?

dialogue.speaker: Usopp
dialogue.text: No, USOPP! Don't get close to it!

dialogue.speaker: Chopper
dialogue.text: We got a winner over here.

dialogue.speaker: Koza
dialogue.text: It's their custom for the loser to become the victor's disciple.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. 1
dialogue.text: No, your stance should be more like this!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: How many disciples do you have already?

dialogue.speaker: Mr. 1
dialogue.text: Alright, let's go to Yuba then!

dialogue.speaker: Baroque Works agent
dialogue.text: We lost a lot of our food supplies because of you!

dialogue.speaker: Mr. 2 Bon Clay
dialogue.text: What would you have done if it weren't for Chopper!?

dialogue.speaker: Koza
dialogue.text: They kept saying they'd accompany us so I had no choice but to convince them with some food.

dialogue.speaker: Mr. 1
dialogue.text: Then I'd just bring them along. What's the problem?

dialogue.speaker: Mr. 2 Bon Clay
dialogue.text: How on earth do you expect to walk into any city if we have a huge flock of dugongs with us?

dialogue.speaker: Baroque Works agent
dialogue.text: In any case, it's quite odd to see dugongs living in the river, Vivi.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: No, they live in the sea.

summary: This part develops the officer agents are expected and pushes the Alabasta conflict closer to its next decisive turn.
